name: Leatherman Wave+ Multi-Tool with Premium Replaceable Wire Cutters

specs.Tools: 18 tools including 3 knives (420HC stainless), needle nose pliers, wire cutters, can opener, saw, scissors, bottle opener, files, screwdrivers, ruler
specs.Outside Access: All blades and tools accessible without opening the pliers (unique to Wave+ tier)
specs.Cutters: Premium replaceable wire cutters — replace without returning tool
specs.Material: 420HC stainless steel with stainless steel handle

whatWorks: The Leatherman Wave+ is the multi-tool that most professional tradespeople, outdoor guides, and serious campers reach for as their everyday carry — not because it's the flashiest option but because it works reliably for decades. The 18 tools cover every realistic field use case: the needle-nose pliers are the heart of any multi-tool and Leatherman's are among the most precise available, the two knives (420HC stainless) hold an edge better than the stamped blades in competing brands, and the saw is actually capable of cutting through small branches rather than just looking the part.

Outside-accessible blades are the Wave+'s defining feature over cheaper multi-tools. The main blade, serrated blade, scissors, and files all open with one hand without first deploying the pliers — you grip the closed tool in your palm and flip a blade open with your thumb. This one-hand deployment is the difference between a tool you reach for habitually and one that stays in your pack. In cold weather with gloved hands, or when one hand is occupied, outside access is not a convenience feature but a practical necessity.

The premium replaceable wire cutters in the Wave+ are a meaningful upgrade over the standard version. Wire cutters wear out from repeated hard use — cutting electrical wire, zip ties, or picture-hanging wire dulls the cutting edge over years. On the standard Wave, worn cutters mean returning the tool to Leatherman for replacement; on the Wave+, you buy replacement cutter inserts and swap them in the field. For contractors, guides, and frequent users, this extends the tool's useful life indefinitely.

Leatherman's 25-year warranty isn't marketing — it's backed by an actual repair and replacement program that the company has honored consistently for decades. 420HC stainless steel is a premium choice for a tool that will encounter moisture, salt air, and hard use: it's significantly more corrosion-resistant than cheaper stainless alloys and holds an edge longer. The overall build quality — tight fit between tools, smooth deployment, no rattle — is noticeably better than multi-tools at half the price.
worthKnowing: The Wave+ weighs 247g and is relatively bulky in a pocket compared to dedicated EDC knives or card-style multi-tools. If weight is critical (ultralight backpacking) or you need something that genuinely disappears in a front pocket (urban carry), the Leatherman Skeletool or a lighter dedicated tool is a better fit. The Wave+ is a do-everything tool that earns its weight in functionality.

The Wave+ does not include a sheath — it comes with a nylon belt pouch that most users find adequate but not elegant. Aftermarket leather sheaths from Leatherman and third parties are available if you prefer a more refined carry option. The belt pouch is included at no extra cost; the leather sheath is a separate purchase.

Canadian border rules: the Wave+ is legal to carry and own in Canada without restriction. Transport considerations: the knife blades are technically prohibited as carry-on items on commercial flights (blade length exceeds TSA/CATSA standards), so pack it in checked luggage when flying.

The Wave+ locking mechanism uses Leatherman's frame lock for the knives — not a liner lock or axis lock. It's a robust, proven mechanism that holds the blade firmly during use, but some knife enthusiasts who prefer specific lock styles will note the difference. For multi-tool use rather than dedicated knife carry, the frame lock is more than adequate.

commonComplaints.title: Heavy and bulky for pocket carry
commonComplaints.body: At 247g, the Wave+ creates significant pocket presence. Daily carry users who want a multi-tool that doesn't weigh them down find lighter alternatives more practical.

commonComplaints.title: No sheath included — just nylon pouch
commonComplaints.body: The included nylon pouch is functional but cheap-feeling relative to the tool quality. Many buyers immediately buy an aftermarket leather sheath.

commonComplaints.title: Prohibited as carry-on on flights
commonComplaints.body: The knife blades are too long for CATSA carry-on rules. Experienced travelers know to pack it in checked luggage, but first-time flyers have had it confiscated.

commonComplaints.title: Tools require two hands to close
commonComplaints.body: While blades open one-handed, closing them back requires a second hand. This is standard for multi-tools but worth knowing if one-handed close is a priority.

verdict: The Leatherman Wave+ is the best all-around multi-tool available — not because it has the most tools or the most exotic materials, but because it executes the fundamentals (pliers, knives, scissors, saw) at a quality level that justifies 25+ years of continuous use. Outside-accessible blades and premium replaceable wire cutters are features that separate it from the competition in daily practice. For anyone who carries a multi-tool seriously — guides, tradespeople, outdoor enthusiasts — it's the default recommendation at this price.

realBuyers.quote: I'm a wilderness guide. This thing goes on every trip. 7 years of heavy use and it's still tight, still sharp, still the tool I reach for before anything else in the kit.
Outside-accessible blades sound like a minor thing until you're trying to cut something one-handed in the cold and realize every other multi-tool needs two hands just to get started.
Bought one for myself in 2017. Bought three more as gifts since then. These are the gifts you actually use. The warranty alone makes it worth the price.
Replaceable wire cutters saved me from sending it back to Leatherman. Bought the insert for $10, swapped it in 5 minutes. Tool is as good as new.

metaDescription: Leatherman Wave+ review: 18 tools, outside-accessible blades, premium replaceable wire cutters, 25-year warranty. Best multi-tool for outdoor use in Canada.
introText: The Leatherman Wave+ Multi-Tool (ASIN B01H5TDHVC) is available on Amazon.ca for $149 CAD — 18 tools in 247g with all knife and scissors blades accessible one-handed without opening the pliers, premium replaceable wire cutters, and a 25-year warranty that makes it a lifetime purchase for guides, tradespeople, and outdoor enthusiasts.
